mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which directly supports corporate revenues and profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns following periods of positive RSAFS growth are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the Rank IC is consistently below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, which reduces consumer confidence and spending, ultimately leading to lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances where the trigger condition is met, or the Rank IC is consistently above -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is persistently high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is actively being raised, it indicates a hawkish monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation, which increases borrowing costs, reduces corporate profitability, and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS increases in 2 of the last 3 months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS does not show a statistically significant negative relationship with returns.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating trend in housing starts (HOUST) during a period of economic recovery signals renewed consumer and business confidence, indicating a broader pickup in economic activity that translates into positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month growth > 5% for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return during triggered periods is not statistically significantly positive (p > 0.05), or if HOUST growth does not correlate positively with returns in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained expansion in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, reflecting healthy corporate operations and demand for goods, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances, or if the Rank IC is consistently below 0.02 during expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a strong and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods of sustained PAYEMS growth, or the Rank IC is consistently below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real GDP (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals a broad and severe contraction in aggregate demand, indicating an economic downturn that will lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon due to immediate market concerns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth is negative AND RSAFS month-over-month growth is negative
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 30% of triggered instances, or if the combined negative signal from GDPC1 and RSAFS does not show a statistically significant negative relationship with returns.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) in an environment of contained inflation indicates an accommodative monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital for businesses and encourages investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0% for 6 consecutive months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances during such regimes, or if the Rank IC is consistently below 0.02.
